summary: The window stays emotionally legible, but the chapter 04/05 handoff drops its new surveillance pressure, chapter 05 replays trust and recognition material the previous chapters already landed, and the chapter 05/06 cut leaves elapsed time unclear.

findings.problem: Chapter 05 establishes an evening operation keyed to Bretz arriving around eight and Damian not escaping Margarethe until well into the reception, then Chapter 06 opens with no time marker at all before later revealing the family dinner is happening at half past eight. The reader has to retroactively infer an unmarked day jump, which makes the opening pages of Chapter 06 feel temporally unstable instead of deliberately reset.
findings.rewrite_direction: Anchor the Chapter 05/06 seam explicitly. Add a clear elapsed-time marker in Chapter 06's first paragraph, or close Chapter 05 with a line that places the next family visit on a later evening.
findings.acceptance_test: Within the first two or three sentences of Chapter 06, a reader can tell whether the family dinner is the next night or later, and the half-past-eight phone call does not force a retrospective timeline correction.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Later chapter owns missing boundary chronology. Chapter 06 creates the confusion by opening on a new domestic scene without telling the reader how much time has passed.

findings.problem: Chapter 04 ends by putting urgent pressure on the exact contact method Damian has just proposed: Brandt is checking the cultural-exchange calendar and Gessler now has Anya's name to pull. Chapter 05 then opens on Damian physically picking Anya up for a joint operation without any orienting line about whether Brandt's check happened, failed, or changed their risk calculus. Read back-to-back, the seam feels like a skipped consequence rather than an intentional ellipsis.
findings.rewrite_direction: Use the first page of Chapter 05 to cash out the Chapter 04 threat. A brief line about the calendar message, the result of Brandt's follow-up, or why this lead is worth escalating to visible in-person contact would carry the surveillance clock forward.
findings.acceptance_test: By the end of Chapter 05's opening page, the reader understands how Damian made first contact after the alliance and whether Brandt/Gessler scrutiny is active, contained, or being consciously risked.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Later chapter owns unresolved boundary pressure. Chapter 05 inherits the Brandt/Gessler surveillance thread and needs to show how the new operation exists under that threat.

findings.problem: Chapter 04 already lands the operative fact that Damian will work with Anya while explicitly withholding trust. Chapter 05 opens by re-establishing the same point in narration and then in dialogue before the club mission can start, so the first car scene spends reader attention reminding us of a tension the previous chapter has already dramatized cleanly.
findings.rewrite_direction: Trim any Chapter 05 lines whose only job is to restate baseline mistrust, and let the opener focus on mission-specific friction: the couple cover, Bretz logistics, and the practical consequences of keeping Anya close.
findings.acceptance_test: In Chapter 05's car scene, each exchange adds new operational pressure, chemistry, or tactical disagreement; no beat exists solely to restate that Damian needs Anya while distrusting her.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Later chapter owns redundant explanation. Chapter 05 can assume the reader remembers the alliance terms Chapter 04 has just established.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:688; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:690; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:702; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:708; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1470; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1472; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1478; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1490
findings.problem: Chapters 03 and 05 push Damian's recognition through almost the same trigger-response sequence: Anya laughs in her unguarded voice, Damian goes still, asks whether they knew each other, and Anya answers too quickly. Chapter 05 escalates the content to Eden and the relay race, but the dramatic machinery is similar enough that the later beat reads as a replay of Chapter 03's scene rather than a fresh turn in their dynamic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Give Chapter 05 a different recognition mechanism or a materially different aftermath. If the laugh stays as the trigger, change the leverage that follows so the scene does more than produce another too-fast denial.
findings.acceptance_test: A reader can describe the Chapter 03 recognition beat and the Chapter 05 recognition beat as distinct scene dynamics, not the same laugh-freeze-question-denial pattern repeated at a higher intensity.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Later chapter owns reused scene dynamics. Chapter 05 repeats a recognition pattern that Chapter 03 has already made legible.
